message:
fix(fr-store): XDEL leaves orphans in PEL + XAUTOCLAIM count budget covers deleted entries (br-frankenredis-r82v)

Two divergences from upstream Redis 7+ surfaced by a new
cross-impl XAUTOCLAIM live-oracle test:

1. XDEL was eagerly removing entries from every consumer-group
   PEL. Upstream t_stream.c::xdelCommand deliberately leaves
   the orphan in PEL so XAUTOCLAIM/XPENDING/XCLAIM can surface
   and clean up the dangling reference. Our pre-cleaning broke
   the `deleted_ids` reply slot of XAUTOCLAIM and made the
   cursor advance past the deleted slot prematurely.

2. XAUTOCLAIM `count` budget. Upstream
   t_stream.c::xautoclaimCommand decrements `count` on BOTH
   claimed and deleted entries (see lines 3441 + 3478) so the
   top-level reply is bounded at `count` regardless of how
   many orphans surface. Our impl only counted claims, which
   diverged on cursor advancement when orphans existed.

- fr-store::xdel: drop the in-loop pending.remove() side effect
- fr-store::xautoclaim: replace `claimed_ids.len() >= count`
  break condition with a unified `budget` counter that
  decrements on both claim and delete events
- fr-store unit test: update
  stream_xautoclaim_claims_entries_by_cursor_and_tracks_deleted_ids
  expectations to match upstream count budgeting (count=2 now
  covers 1 claim + 1 delete; cursor stops at the next PEL id)
- fr-conformance: new live_redis_xautoclaim_matches_upstream
  cross-impl test driving both runtimes through the same
  XADD/XGROUP/XREADGROUP/XACK/XDEL/XAUTOCLAIM sequence and
  comparing replies slot-by-slot (cursor + claimed-entries +
  deleted-ids) for COUNT, JUSTID, MINIDLETIME paths

fr-store 288 passed; fr-runtime 280 passed; fr-command 652
passed; fr-conformance 184 passed; cargo clippy --tests
-D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean.

Closes br-frankenredis-r82v.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
